methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, founded by Thomas Youmans and Thomas Callen. The comparable universe consists of 7 private companies operating in drones and autonomous systems, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comps exist in this narrow segment.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The sector contains only 5 same-sector peers, and filtering by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints in any filtered bucket. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and applications across the broader drones and autonomous space.

Without viable triangulation via peer comparables, this analysis cannot produce a defensible valuation range based on the available data. A complete valuation would require either: (1) expansion to broader comparable sets with explicit adjustment justification, (2) revenue or unit economics multiples if the target has disclosed financial performance, or (3) application of venture capital method using explicit assumptions about exit scenarios and probability-weighted returns. The key uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient narrow-band comparables at seed stage in this frontier sector.
